why_it_matters:
Released today: /loop

/loop is a powerful new way to schedule recurring tasks, for up to 3 days at a time

eg. “/loop babysit all my PRs. Auto-fix build issues and when comments come in, use a worktree agent to fix them”

eg. “/loop every morning use the Slack MCP to give me a summary of top posts I was tagged in”

Let us know what you think!

why_it_matters:
This is one of the wildest AI release this week.

Andrej Karpathy just dropped an open-source tool where AI agents improve LLM training by themselves.

> You'll need One GPU.
> perform 5-minute experiments.
> Agents will auto-optimizing architectures, hyperparameters, everything.

AI is now literally doing AI research.

title: My agent setup: 4 Claude Code agents in tmux, 2x2 grid on the left, shared preview panel on the right. It's the only thing I'm looking at on my computer. No more software, no more UI.   One bash script launches everything: tmux-work  The trick: in CLAUDE.md, I tell every agent ab
source: twitter:search:"CLAUDE.md" (tip OR trick OR setup OR config)
why_it_matters:
My agent setup: 4 Claude Code agents in tmux, 2x2 grid on the left, shared preview panel on the right. It's the only thing I'm looking at on my computer. No more software, no more UI. 

One bash script launches everything: tmux-work

The trick: in CLAUDE.md, I tell every agent about the preview pane:

"Panel %1 is the tmux preview shell. To show a document, run: tmux send-keys -t %1 'q' C-m 'preview "path/to/file"' C-m"

Now when I say "preview this file", Claude pushes it to the right panel whi

title: The usability imperative for securing digital asset devices
why_it_matters: When Tony Fadell started working on the iPod, usability often trumped security. The result was an iterative process. Every time someone would find a security weakness or a way to hack the device, the development group would iterate to add measures and fix the issues.
